data.title: Siemens USA President & CEO Barbara Humpton to retire on Oct 1, 2025 after 14 years, Ann Fairchild appointed interim CEO.
data.description: Siemens has revealed that Barbara Humpton, President and CEO of Siemens USA, will retire from the company on October 1, 2025. Ann Fairchild, who currently serves as the General Counsel for Siemens USA, will take over as the interim President and CEO starting from the same date. Humpton has spent 14 years with Siemens, out of which seven were as the U.S. CEO. She played a vital role in solidifying Siemens’ status as a prominent technology firm in the United States. Fairchild is set to assume the leadership role temporarily following Humpton’s retirement.

data.title: Multiple candidates are being considered for the next CFTC chair position, including those supportive of crypto.
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is currently without a permanent chair. However, there are reportedly multiple potential candidates who support cryptocurrency that are being considered to lead the agency.

data.title: VIOFO introduces A229 Ultra Series: first 4K dual dashcam with 210° cabin view. Upgrade from A229 Pro.
data.description: Shenzhen, China, as of September 26, 2025 - VIOFO has recently unveiled the A229 Ultra Series, a significant upgrade from the A229 Pro Series. This new model boasts dual 4K front and rear cameras along with a Full HD fisheye cabin camera. The A229 Ultra Series marks a substantial advancement in resolution and cabin coverage compared to its predecessor.

data.title: 360Quadrants recognizes top startups and SMEs in Game-based Learning in 2025, with a focus on market presence and product innovation.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, a report was released on September 26, 2025, by 360Quadrants focusing on Game-based Learning for Startups/SMEs in 2025. The report highlighted various key players in the industry, spanning global leaders and up-and-coming companies. These entities were acknowledged for their outstanding performance in terms of market visibility, introduction of new products, and operational achievements. The report showcased the diversity and expertise present in the sector, showcasing the successes of various players in this dynamic field.

data.title: Maize market to reach US$274.32 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 3.2%. Demand from feed and biofuel industries rising.
data.description: In a recent report released by The Insight Partners on September 26, 2025, it was revealed that the global maize market is experiencing steady growth. This growth is primarily attributed to rising demand from various sectors including animal feed, industrial uses, and biofuel production. The analysis conducted provides a comprehensive overview of the current trends in the industry. The report emphasizes the increasing importance of maize in these sectors, showcasing its significance in the market. The data presented underscores the positive trajectory of the global maize market, with significant uptrends in demand and utilization across different industries.

data.title: VinFast VF 9 redefines prestige in Canada's EV market. 8.6% of new vehicles are zero-emission, Quebec leads with 11%.
data.description: In Markham, Ontario, prestige is redefined as clean lines, calm power, and bold elegance in the world of electric vehicles. Despite the growth in electric vehicle sales in Canada, adoption rates are inconsistent. As of Q2 2025, zero-emission vehicles accounted for approximately 8.6% of new vehicle registrations across the country. Quebec stands out with a share exceeding 11%. In this landscape, a top-tier electric SUV must offer more than just style.
